para_trans: Regarding sanction of expenditure under the Scheme for Promotion of Cashew Processing Industry for the year 2025-26.
Government of Maharashtra
Industries, Energy, Labour and Mining Department
Government Decision No: - Kaproyo 2025 / Q. No.199 / Udyog-2
Mantralaya, Mumbai - 400 032.
Date: 27th March, 2026
Reference: -

1) Government Decision No. CAPRPO-2010 / Pt. No.548 / Udyog-2, dated 15/07/2010 2) Government Circular No. CAPRO-2010 / PR. No.548 / Udyog-2, dated 18/02/2011 3) Government Decision No. No.136 / Udyog-2, dated 12/03/2013 4) Government Decision No. Kaprayo-2020 / Q.78 / Udyog-2, dated 02/12/2020 5) Government Decision No. No.78 / Udyog-2, dated 09/06/2021 6) Government Decision No. No.119 / Udyog-2, dated 04/07/2025 7) Letter from Additional Director of Industries, Directorate of Industries, Mantralaya, Mumbai No. Sugarcane / Saproyo / Capryo / Grant / C-9959, d. 06.11.2025.
8) Letter from Additional Director of Industries, Directorate of Industries, Mantralaya, Mumbai No. Sugarcane / Saproyo / Kapryo / Subsidy / 2024 / A-956, dated 14/11/2024.
9) Letter from Additional Director of Industries, Directorate of Industries, Mantralaya, Mumbai No. Sugarcane / Saprio / Caprio / Grant / 2025 O-88, dated 05/02/2025.
The Government's Decisions:

The government has decided to provide incentives to the cashew processing industry in the state. The decision has been taken by the government of 15/07/2010. As well as d. The Government decision of 12/03/2013 has approved continuation of the scheme by fixing a revised mechanism for allocation of incentive subsidy to the cashew processing industry. Under the scheme, the amount of the tax was provided to the concerned entities as incentive subsidy after payment of 5 per cent value-added tax payable on the sale price of the cashews finally sold in the state out of the cashews produced and processed in the state. However, d. A new system of levying Goods and Services Tax (GST) instead of Value Added Tax started from 01/07/2017. In the light of this tax structure. Under the government decision of 02/12/2020, the system of distribution of incentive grants under the scheme has been reformed. Cashew nuts finally sold in the state are taxed at 5% Goods and Services Tax (GST) at 5551 '(2. 5%) and CGST (2. 4 SHH). The gross state goods and services tax (SGST) is the percentage of gross state goods and services tax (SGST) paid by the industrial units after they pay GST. The cashew processing industry has been permitted as an incentive subsidy from the 01/04/2020. In order to settle the pending claims under the scheme, the budget of the year 2025-26 has allocated Rs. 7.00 crore out of which Rs. 4.20 crores. As per the Government Decision dated 04.07.2025, the remaining funds i.e. Rs. The government was considering disbursing Rs 2.8 crore to the concerned cashew processing units.

2. The budgetary provision available on the BIMSTEC system to settle a total of 47 pending claims under the Scheme for Promotion of Cashew Processing Industry (2852 0481) is Rs. The balance of the 7.00 crore corpus i.e. Rs. It has been approved to disburse funds to the tune of ₹2.80 crore.
3. Considering the funds available on the BDS system, the Government mentioned in Reference No. 3 Wb 4
Verification of provisions, criteria, qualifications, etc. in the decision / circulars / ensuring strict implementation of the condition
Subject to the condition of making, the available Rs. 2,80,00, 000 / - (Rupees Two Crore UM Lakhs only)
A total of 47 cashew processing industries with 18 claims in statement (a) and 29 claims in statement (b) are listed below.
The distribution of components is being approved by this government decision.

be subtracted from the provision.

For a total of 47 claims mentioned in Paragraph 3 above, the amount received is Rs. Withdrawal to the Accounts Officer, Directorate of Industries, Mumbai for withdrawal and disbursement of amount 2,80,00, 000 / - (Rupees Two Crore Eighty Lakh Lakhs only) and

The Distributing Officer, as well as the Additional Director of Industries, Directorate of Industries, Mumbai, is being declared as the Controlling Officer. They deposit the above amount into their personal virtual deposit account by submitting the payment to the treasury.
to be deposited in (VPDA).

6. Incentives to be granted under the Incentive Scheme to the Cashew Processing Industry from the amount deposited in the above mentioned Personal Virtual Deposit Account should be disbursed as mentioned in the above mentioned paragraph 3.
7. The Utilization Certificate of the amount sanctioned under this Government Decision shall be furnished by the Directorate of Industries, Mumbai to the Government forthwith.
Do it.
8. This Government decision is an informal reference to the Department of Finance 1504 / Expenditure-16, d. of the authorization granted under 10/03/2026.
It is being issued accordingly.
